stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model.
It is categorized under key ML/AI topics including transformers and text-generation.
The source, Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicates its relevance within the open-source AI community.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The source, Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicates this is a community/model tracking update rather than a breakthrough announcement.

stories.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA has selected Relativity Space, led by Eric Schmidt, for a 2028 Mars mission.
The partnership involves Relativity Space providing the full launch and cruise operations for the Aeolus payload.
This represents a significant public-private partnership in deep space exploration.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has discovered a new, lightweight backdoor malware dubbed 'Crypto Clipper'.
The malware is capable of stealing cryptocurrency from infected systems.
It spreads via USB drives and utilizes the Tor network for communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning language models on insecure code can cause emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and validates an 'actionable activation-space direction' that correlates with this misalignment across diverse model architectures.
A difference-in-means direction achieved extremely high separation (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations in four different instruction-tuned model families.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its capability in conversational AI.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the bigscience/bloom model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies, including transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model outputs.
The updates also lead to faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Apple devices.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The Atlantic has made four datasets of music used to train AI models publicly searchable.
Two of the discovered music datasets are massive, containing 12 million and 9 million tracks respectively.
This highlights the growing public scrutiny and accessibility of the data fueling AI music generation.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of administrative controversy involving a Trump official's refusal to review the vaccine.
The story highlights the regulatory and political hurdles in major pharmaceutical approvals.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for national defense.
The increased drone spending is also expected to benefit Taiwanese businesses internationally.
The context of escalating tensions with China is driving this defense buildup.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service.
A rollout of this new system service is scheduled for the current month.
Significant changes related to this verification are set to begin in September.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses a key limitation in state-of-the-art Flow-Matching TTS: static performance after deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that allows pronunciation corrections without retraining the core model.
The method works by learning corrections as latent conditioning edits within the text embedding space, rather than updating model weights.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces 'StylisticBias,' a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research by separating appearance-based effects from genuine identity differences in bias assessment.
Focuses on how subtle human visual cues drive social biases within increasingly consequential AI systems.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper addresses a major limitation in LLM alignment: the high cost and scarcity of explicit human feedback.
It proposes using implicit user feedback (like mouse movements or gaze tracking) to train LLM reward models.
This approach aims to leverage rich, readily available behavioral data, similar to how internet giants build economic moats.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, a popular local LLM runner.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models without relying on cloud services.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Philips Hue introduced wired wall modules to integrate non-smart lights into the Hue ecosystem.
The new modules are designed to be installed behind existing wall switches.
Hue also launched more affordable versions of its Signe series lamps and upgraded its E14 range.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK government plans to implement facial scanning technology for age verification of asylum seekers.
Tests have demonstrated that the age-verification technology carries a significant risk of making life-altering errors.
The deployment proceeds despite awareness of the technology's known flaws.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has instructed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves reassigning the majority of the affected employees to other ongoing programs.
This signals a potential shift or change in NASA's lunar exploration hardware strategy.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, a model that operates significantly in a continuous latent space.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces cross-attention attribution, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers of speech diffusion models to improve controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where state must be reconstructed from the prompt across turns, leading to inefficiency.
Focuses on improving policy-adherence in tool-calling agents, particularly for complex customer-service applications.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: Introduces DeepSWIP, a new method to handle counterfactual reasoning in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
Addresses the limitation of standard associational inference by providing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
Achieves this by using neural materialization to map fixed-context neural predicates to ProbLog choices and applying SWIPs.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: Introduces SARLO-80, a new, large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
Addresses a critical limitation in multimodal AI by providing high-resolution SAR data that preserves complex-valued measurements and native acquisition geometry.
Aims to enable more physically grounded multimodal learning for foundation models, which currently lack comparable high-quality SAR benchmarks to optical data.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of the LiveCodeBench (LCB) benchmark.
LCB is a recognized benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, currently limited to Python.
Multi-LCB aims to improve LLM evaluation by extending the benchmark to support multiple programming languages.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is deemed critical for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Proposes 'privacy via predictability' as an alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
This new framework offers a fine-grained privacy measure that accounts for the attacker's specific knowledge.
It aims to mitigate the costly privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in worst-case DP guarantees.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with high-throughput KV cache methods.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and tight responsiveness.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in LLM coding agents: the lack of high-level operational knowledge about a codebase (e.g., subsystem locations, testing procedures).
The paper investigates the effectiveness of using supplementary context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide these coding agents.
The work proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine' tuning method to improve repository guidance, aiming to resolve conflicting findings in prior studies.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for policy-adherent AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly tracking state across tool calls and policy adherence.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual dataset (1.2M pairs) of CT/MR images and text for radiology.
Develops RefRad2D to train spatially grounded Vision-Language Models (VLMs) without manual spatial annotations.
The resulting model, RadGrounder, performs multiple tasks including report generation and visual question answering.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a common challenge in online content moderation.
Proposes a new dataset, 'CATCH-ME if you RAG,' to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for training LLMs.
Aims to improve the generation of effective, non-repetitive counterspeech responses using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) techniques.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for making large models scalable and cost-effective.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to integrate psychometrics into Automated Essay Scoring (AES).
Addresses the limitation of current systems where scoring and instructional feedback are treated as separate, disconnected components.
Aims to provide both reliable assessment and actionable, proficiency-aware feedback, overcoming LLM insensitivity to learner levels.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF), a nine-dimension schema for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
Argues that current AI models fail on Nigerian discourse due to 'context failure,' not just translation issues.
Challenges existing sentiment benchmarks (like NaijaSenti) which only use simple three-way polarity classification.

stories.title: AI Agent Blind Spot Detector: Find Failed Conversations Before They Become Churn
stories.author: jackm-singularity
bullet_points: Proposes an AI agent blind spot detector to preempt customer churn.
The solution utilizes unresolved-intent clustering and outcome scoring.
It incorporates trace evidence, review queues, and product feedback loops for continuous improvement.

stories.title: The ₹0 Automation Stack: Enterprise-Grade Workflows Without Paying for SaaS
stories.author: automate-archit
bullet_points: The article discusses building enterprise-grade automation workflows without relying on expensive SaaS solutions.
It highlights a real-world case study where a textile exporter was incurring significant monthly SaaS costs.
The core theme is cost-effective, self-hosted, or open-source automation implementation.
